title: CHIEF ELECTORAL OFFICER HOVIN ELECTORAL ROLL OBSERVER THUKHAWM
content:
1053/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 17th June, 2026:Vawiin Khan Chief Electoral Officer Pi Garima Gupta, IAS hovin SIR (2026) kalpui meka Electoral Roll Observer ten Secretariat Conference Hall, MINECO ah Electoral Roll Observer te tihtur leh hriattur te zir hona hun an hmang.

CEO chuan ECI duhdan ang ngeia Electoral Roll dik leh felfai kan neih theihnana ruat an nih thu ERO te hrilhin, electoral roll thar hlak buatsaih a nih avangin ERO te chu fimkhur taka hna thawk turin a chah a. Final roll chhuah hma ngeia an district chan theuh te chu vawithum tal an tlawh chhuah a tul thu a sawi a. Mipui ten harsatna an neih ang te ngaihthlak sak turin a chah bawk a ni. ERO te hian harsatna an nei a nih chuan CEO office in an puih theih reng thu a sawi bawk.

Khawzawl Election Officer, State Level Master Trainer ni bawk Timothy R. Vanlalhmangaiha,  chuan Electoral Roll Observer  te  tihtur tul Powerpoint Presentation hmangin a zirtir a. Mizoramin SIR-a kan thlen chin te tarlangin, SIR kalpui mek state-a a hmahruaitu kan nih thu a sawi a. Electoral Roll Observer  ten an hriat duh te zawtin, sawihona hun an hmang zui nghal bawk a ni.

Electoral Roll Observer atana ruat te chu, Pu K. Lalthawmmawia ( Commisioner & Secretary DP&AR / UD & PA), Pi Lalzarmawii ( Commisioner & Secretary Social Welfare, Women & Child Development ), Dr. Lalrozama ( Commissioner & Secretary Higher & Technical Education ), Pu V.Lalsangliana ( Commisioner & Secretary Sports & Youth Services), Pi Zodingpuii ( Secretary, Art & Culture / PHE ), Dr. Lalhriatzuali Ralte ( Secretary LESDE/ CEO, Mizoram Health Care Society) te an ni.

Attachment

title: UNNATI 2024 Capacity Building Workshop neih a ni
content:
1052/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 17 June 2026:Vawiin khan Commerce, Industries, Geology & Mining Department chuan  I&PR Conference Hall, Treasury Square, Aizawl-ah UNNATI 2024 Capacity Building Workshop a buatsaih a.  Pu Lalhmunsiama, Additional Director, Directorate of Commerce & Industries chuan Workshop hi a kaihruai a ni.

Workshop-ah hian DPIIT team leader Pu Rahul Saini, Section Officer hnen atangin UNNATI Scheme thil tum leh a kalphungte  ngaihthlak a ni a, Mizorama UNNATI kalpui dan chungchang report ngaihthlak a nih bakah, DPIIT Portal Team chuan UNNATI Portal hman dan leh application thehluh dan chungchang a taka entirna (live demonstration) an nei a. Hemi hnuah bank aiawh te, stakeholders te leh participants te atanga zawhna leh chhanna hun buatsaih nghal a ni.

Vawiin Workshop-ah hian Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce & Industries, Government of India aiawh mi panga (5) an rawn tel thei a ni.

Attachment

title: Mizoram Governor Graces Joint Celebration of the Foundation Days of Telangana and West Bengal
content:
Aizawl, the 17th June, 2026:Mizoram Governor, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd), graced the joint celebration of the Foundation Days of Telangana and West Bengal this afternoon. The event was organised under the 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' initiative at the Durbar Hall, Lok Bhavan.

In his address, Governor General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh highlighted the significance of the Union Government’s flagship programme, 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' (One India, Great India), in fostering greater unity and harmony among the diverse communities of India. He noted that India’s rich diversity - evident in its languages, cultures, traditions, attire, cuisine, and religious practices - is the country’s unique strength and must be cherished and preserved. He encouraged the people of Mizoram, particularly the youth and students, to learn more about the cultures and traditions of other states and to make efforts to acquire proficiency in other Indian languages.

The Governor praised both states for their rich cultural heritage. He described West Bengal as one of India’s oldest and most illustrious states, known for producing numerous scientists, cinema personalities, writers, poets, musicians and revolutionaries. He specifically mentioned that India’s National Anthem and National Song, which inspire strong nationalism among citizens, originated from Bengal. Speaking on Telangana, the Governor said that despite being one of India's youngest states, it has a rich historical and cultural heritage. He praised the hardworking nature of its people and noted that Hyderabad has emerged as a major IT hub of the country.

The programme was hosted by Mrs. Lalhruaitluangi, Additional Private Secretary to the Governor. A video message from Telangana Governor Shri Shiv Pratap Shukla was screened, followed by the presentation of videos highlighting the rich cultural heritage and tourism potential of both Telangana and West Bengal. Insightful speeches were delivered by Dr. Muttu Vemula from Telangana, Assistant Professor in the Department of Education, Mizoram University and Dr. Arnab Roy from West Bengal, Assistant Professor in the Department of Food Technology, Mizoram University.

Students from Mizoram University, Pachhunga University College and the Centre for Disaster Management added vibrancy to the occasion through colourful cultural performances. Students from Synod Higher Secondary School also participated in the event as invitees. After the programme concluded, the Governor interacted with the attendees at Upper Lawn, Lok Bhavan.

Telangana, India’s youngest state, was formed on 2 June, 2014, while West Bengal came into existence on 20 June, 1947.

The 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' programme aims to promote stronger bonds and deeper understanding among the people of India’s diverse states and Union Territories by encouraging mutual appreciation of their unique cultures, traditions, and ways of life. In Mizoram, this initiative has been observed under the Governor’s leadership since June 2023, with the celebration of Foundation Days of various states and Union Territories at Lok Bhavan.

Attachment

title: Larsap hovin Telangana leh West Bengal State-te dinchamphaphak lawm
content:
1051/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 17th June, 2026:Mizoram Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd) hovin vawiin chawhnu khan 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' kaltlangin Telangana leh West Bengal State-te dinchamphaphak lawmna Durbar Hall, Lok Bhavan-ah neih a ni.

Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh chuan India rama cheng hnam hrang hrangte inkara inpumkhatna leh inunauna tha zawk a awm theih nana Sawrkar laipui flagship programme "Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat (One India, Great India)' chu hlut thiam a pawimawh thu a sawi a. India ram hmun tinah tawng, hnam ziarang, nunphung, incheina, ei leh in, sakhua leh adangteah danglamna nasa tak a awm thu sawiin, "Kan danglamna hi kan chakna thuruk leh khawvel ang pawha hmuh tur vang tak a ni a, hengte hi kan hlut thiama kan vawnnun tlat a tul a ni, a ti a. Kalkhawmte chu state dangte hnam ziarang leh nunphungte zira hriat chian lehzual tum turin a chah a. A bikin Mizo zirlaite chu hnam dangte tawng thiam tum turin a chah bawk

Larsap chuan State pahnih ten hnam ziarang hausa tak an neih thu sawiin, West Bengal chu state hmasa leh ropui tak a nih thu sawiin, scientist, ziakmi, hla phuah thiam, music leh cinema lama mi hmasa bakah khawtlang leh ram siam that kawnga mi bikte lo chhuahna a nih thu a sawi a. Ram leh hnam rilru kan put tlan theih nana National Anthem leh National Song tha tak takte lo chhuahna a nih thu a sawi bawk. Telangana chu state naupangte ni mahse, hnam ziarang leh nunphung hausa tak nei a nih thu sawiin, mipuite an taimak em avangin Hyderabad chu India rama Information Technology hmunpui pawimawh tak a nih thu a sawi bawk.

He hun hi Pi Lalhruaitluangi, Additional Private Secretary to Governor chuan kaihruaiin programme buatsaih chhan a sawifiah a. Telangana Larsap Pu Shiv Pratap Shukla thuchah bakah heng state pahnihte chanchin tarlanna video thlir ho a ni a. Telangana aiawh Dr. Muttu Vemula, Asst. Professor, Department of Education, Mizoram University leh West Bengal aiawh Dr. Arnab Roy, Assistant Professor, Department of Food and Technology, Mizoram University-te hnen atangin thusawi ngaihthlak a ni a. Mizoram University, Pachhunga University leh Centre for Disaster Management zirlai ten hnam ziarang lan theinna item benglut tak tak an entir a. Sawm an nih angin Synod Higher Secondary School zirlaite pawh he hunah hian an tel bawk. Programme hman zawh hian Larsap chuan he ni puala kalkhawmte chu Upper Lawn, Lok Bhavan-ah a kawm a ni.

Telangana hi India rama state naupang ber niin June ni 2, 2014 khan state puitlinga hlankai a ni a, West Bengal hi June ni 20, 1947 khan a lo piang a ni.

Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat programme hian India rama State leh UT hrang hranga cheng hnam hrang hrang, hnam ziarang leh nunphung inang lo tak takte inkara inpawhna tha zawk siam leh an inkara inhriatthiam tawnna nasa zawk siam a tum a. Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat programme kaltlang hian India rama State leh UT hrang hrangte dinchamphaphak lawmna chu Lok Bhavan, Mizoramah Larsap kaihhruaiin June, 2023 atang khan buatsaih tan a ni.

Attachment

title: AIZAWL DC ORDER REGARDING SINGLE USE PLASTIC AT TOURIST SITES
content:
179/2026-2027

17th June, 2026:In pursuance of the Order dated 05.05.2026 of the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India in C.A. No. 6174/2023 regarding implementation of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026, which directed the identification of tourist sites and strengthening of waste management measures at such locations, and in accordance with Memo No. C-16/2/2026-UD&PA dated 01.06.2026 issued by the Urban Development & Poverty Alleviation Department, Government of Mizoram, the following tourist sites have been identified in Aizawl District:

1. Lalsavunga Park2. Falkawn Zokhua3. Sakawrhmuituai Tlang4. Hmuifang Tlang5. Khawhpawp, Lawipu6. Sairang Railway Station7. Sialsuk Tlang

In order to prevent littering and plastic pollution and to ensure effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026, the use, sale, distribution and storage of Single-Use Plastic (SUP) items within the above-mentioned tourist sites are hereby prohibited with immediate effect.

All visitors, vendors, shopkeepers and other stakeholders are directed to use environmentally friendly and reusable alternatives. Compliance with this Order shall be ensured by the Nodal Officer and Sanitation Officer on Single-Use Plastic Enforcement through the SUP Enforcement Team.

Any violation of this Order shall attract action under the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026, the Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016, and other applicable laws and regulations.

title: Minister Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar, Aizawl West-II bialtu MLA chuan school naupang uniform pheikhawk sem
content:
1050/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 17th of June, 2026:Vawiin chawhnu dar 1:00 khan Minister Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar, Sports & Youth Services Department etc. changtu, Aizawl West II bialtu MLA ni bawk chuan a bial chhunga sorkar primary school a zirlaite hnenah school uniform pheikhawk a sem a. Govt. Primary School, Dinthar leh Govt. Gairigaon Primary School te hnenah pheikhawk semna hun hi Dinthar YMA Hall-ah hman a ni.

Minister Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar chuan, “Aizawl West-II a veng hrang hranga sorkar primary school-ah school uniform pheikhawk hi kan sem dawn a, kan bial chhunga sorkar primary school zirlai zawng zawngten an dawng vek ang. Sorkar school a lutte kan puihna tura sem kan ni a, kumtharah engtinnge kan inpuih leh theih ang tih pawh kan ngaihtuah leh dawn,” a ti.

Mizoram a zirtirtu tha berte awmna chu sorkar school a ni a, sorkar school-ah chuan trained teacher vek an awm a. Nimahsela a pawl hniam lamah sorkar school-a in kaltir hrehna a awm thin a, Aizawlah sorkar school te hi luh nan a tha tawk a ni tih hi Aizawl West II atang hriattir kan duh, Minister chuan a ti a. A bial chhungah zirna tihhmasawn a tihlen zel a tum thu leh hma an sawn ve zel ni a a hriat thu sawiin Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar chuuan Dinthar Vengah sawrkar sikul hmunhma in a zawh loh avangin zirlai pawh inlak theih loh chin a awm a, sikul tihchangtlun lamah pawh hmalak dan a ngaihtuah tur thu a sawi.

LESDE Minister ni bawk Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar chuan zirlai nu leh pate, nitina inhlawha eizawngte chu Mizoram Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWWB) a inziaklut turin a sawm a. MBOCWWB member fate zirnaah hamthatna hrang hrang an ban phakah a awm thu a sawi. LESDE Office-ah thlakhata cheng 10 chawia inziahluh theih reng a nih thu sawiin, an faten zirna sang zawk an kai thleng pawha hamthatna dawn tur a awm thu te, dam lohna thilahte leh thildangah pawh hamthatna dawn tur a awm tih a sawi a ni. MBOCWWB hnuaiah Rahbi Chhertu leh Eizawnna Kailawn kaltlangin thiamna hrang hrang zir theih a nih bakah bul tanna tur thlengin a inngaihtuahpui theih thu a sawi tel nghal a ni.

Bialtu MLA in school uniform pheikhawk a semnaah hian a thawhkhatnaah Dinthar Venga sorkar primary school pahnihte atangin zirlai 214 in pheikhawk an dawng a. Tunkar leh karleh tirlamah sem chhunzawm a ni dawn a, bial chhung primary school zirlai 673 hnenah pheikhawk hi pek turin an ruahman.

Vawiin hun hmanaah hian Pi Carolyn Rinthanpuii, SDEO, Aizawl West leh Dinthar Local Council te bakah zirlai naupangte, zirtirtute leh nu leh pate an tel a ni.

title: TUICHANG BIALTU MLA PU W. CHHUANAWMA’N KHAWZAWL DISTRICT HOSPITAL TAN WORLD CLASS DENTAL CHAIR PE
content:
1048/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 17th June, 2026:Tuichang Bialtu MLA Pu W. Chhuanawma chuan MLA Fund hmangin Khawzawl District mipuite hman turin Dental Chair tha tak a lei a, he Dental Chair hi a PA, Pi Lalnunthari chuan Khawzawl District mipuite hman theih turin a hawng. Hawnna inkhawmah hian Pi Lalnunthari chuan thusawiin, Tuichang Bialtu MLA Pu W. Chhuanawma hian a bial mipuite hriselna a ngaipawimawh em avangin he Dental Chair hi a MLA Fund hmanga a lei thu a sawi.

Tuichang North Block President Pu Zodinsanga pawhin thusawiin, ‘Tuichang Bialtu MLA Pu W. Chhuanawma hi damlohnain tlakbuak hle mahse, Khawzawl Damdawiin hi a ngaipawimawh em em a, Dental chair a lei mai piah lamah hian Damdawiin kawt zawl hi Alkatra hmanga siam turin PWD lamah a nawr nghal a, damdawiin Canteen leh damdawi zawrhna pawh a building sak thuai a nih theih nan hmalak mek a ni’ a ti a. Khawzawl damdawiin wiring leh earth line hman theih mumal a nih loh avangin bialtu MLA hmalaknain hman theih turin siam that vek a ni bawk a. Khawzawl damdawiin Staff-te nen an thawhhona a tha a, an chungah lawmthu a sawi bawk a ni.

title: KHAWZAWL KVK-IN KHET BACHAO ABHIYAN CAMPAIGN BUATSAIH
content:
1047/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 17th June, 2026:Vawiin khan KVK Khawzawl chuan KVK Training hall-ah Khet Bachao Abhiyan (Safe the field campaign) hun an hmang a, Dr. Malsawmkimi, Senior Scientist & Head, KVK Khawzawl chuan a kaihruai.

He hunah hian Khawzawl khawchhung Darngawn leh Hermon veng loneitute hnenah Pu Rambuatsaiha, SMS (Agronomy) chuan lei tha hman dan dik, dawmdawi leh hlo tur tellova thlai enkawl dan leh awmze nei zawka huan thlai enkawl dan zirtirna a pe a. Natural Farming hnuaia loneitute hnenah hian lei tih thatna Jeevamit, Ghanajeevamrit leh rannung laka thlai venna leh enkawlna Neemastra siam dan leh a hman zirtirna neih a ni bawk.

title: “Cleanliness and Hygiene Enhance Mizoram’s Reputation” – Chief Minister
content:
178/2026-2027

Aizawl, 17 June 2026:The Formosa Foundation today donated a Garbage Tipper to Joint YMA Thenzawl in support of efforts to maintain cleanliness and improve waste management in the town. Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma attended the handover programme as the Chief Guest, accompanied by Pi Baryl Vanneihsangi, MLA.

The programme was held at the Joint YMA Hall, Bazar Veng, Thenzawl.

Addressing the gathering, the Chief Minister said that one of the most common compliments received from visitors to Mizoram is the State’s cleanliness and well-maintained environment. He remarked that cleanliness and hygiene enhance the reputation of both Mizoram and its people, and urged citizens to continue prioritising these values.

The Chief Minister noted that Thenzawl has emerged as one of Mizoram’s most prominent tourist destinations and is increasingly attracting visitors from across the country. Given its growing popularity and scenic appeal, he stressed the importance of maintaining high standards of cleanliness in the town. He stated that the donation of the Garbage Tipper by the Formosa Foundation would further strengthen local efforts to keep Thenzawl clean and attractive.

He also expressed gratitude to the Formosa Foundation for its continued contributions to the development and welfare of Mizoram.

Speaking on the occasion, Pi Baryl Vanneihsangi, MLA, said that cleanliness contributes not only to public health but also to the attractiveness of a place. As Thenzawl receives a large number of travellers and tourists, she emphasised the importance of maintaining a clean environment that can serve as a model for other towns and communities. She further underlined the importance of proper waste management in achieving this objective.

The programme was chaired by Pu Lalmuanpuia, President of Joint YMA Thenzawl. The welcome address was delivered by Pu Samuel Lalrinpuia, Secretary of Vantawng Group YMA. Pu Jonathan Lalremruata, Chairman of Formosa Foundation, also addressed the gathering. The handover of the Garbage Tipper was conducted under the guidance of Mr Clinton Sherwood, Legal Advisor of the Formosa Foundation.

Attachment

title: KHAWZAWLAH DCC, DLRC & DLSC MEETING NEI
content:
1046/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 17th June, 2026:Vawiin khan Khawzawl District Consultative Committee, District Level Review Committee leh District Level Security Committee for quarter ended March 2026 chu an Chairman, Bawrhsap Pu K. Vanlalthiana kaihhruaiin DC Conference Hall-ah an thukhawm.Chairman Pu K. Vanlalthiana chuan member kalkhawmte chu an rawngbawlsak mipuite hre reng chunga hna thawk zel turin a fuih a. Bank-te chu mipuite abikin thingtlang mite khaichhuah nan leh eizawnna kawnga hma an sawn theih nan tanpuia hma lak pui zel turin a chah bawk.Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Lead Bank Manager Pu Lawmkima chuan agenda sawizauna a nei a. January – March, 2026 (4th Quarter, 2025–2026) chhunga Khawzawl District-a bank hrang hrangte hna thawh thlir let niin, hmachhawp an neih leh hma lak zui dan tur te sawi ho a ni.He hunah hian bank sum vawn himna (security) lam leh pawisa leng vel (currency management & remittance) chungchang sawi ho a ni a. January – March 2026 chhung khan Khawzawl mipuite zingah sawrkarin social security scheme a ruahman PMSBY-ah mi 2275, PMJJBY-ah mi 1522 leh APY-ah mi 225 an inziaklut a. Inziaklut tam zawk an awm theih nan ṭan lak lehzual ni se, tih a ni a. Bank, Lead Bank leh CFL kaltlangin mipui hnenah sum hman leh enkawl dan inzirtirna (Financial Literacy Campaign) ṭum 27 buatsaih niin mi 1058-in an chhim tih tarlan a ni a. FLC hi ṭha taka kalpui chhunzawm zel turin bank-te'n ṭan la tlang se tih a ni bawk.Bank-a mipuiin sum an dah luh (deposit) zat za zela bank-in mipui hnena sum an puktir (credit) zat chhutna, Credit/Deposit Ratio (CD Ratio)-ah March Quarter 2026 chhungin CD Ratio 80.41% an nei a, Mizoram district zawng zawng zingah Khawzawl district chu a ti tha ber pathumna a ni.He hunah hian kum 2026–2027 sawrkar kum chhunga Khawzawl District chhunga bank-te'n loan an pek chhuah dan tur ruahmanna, Annual Credit Plan (ACP) chu Chairman Pu K. Vanlalthiana chuan a tlangzarh nghal a. Sum pek chhuah tura tinzawn chu Priority Sector Agriculture-ah loan cheng nuai 8631.88, Priority Sector MSME-ah loan cheng nuai 2079.01, Other Priority Sector Loan-ah cheng nuai 2485.66 leh Non-Priority Sector-ah loan cheng nuai 2733, a zavaia belh khawmin loan cheng nuai 15,929.55 a ni.Vawiin hunah hian SDO, Line Department, RBI, NABARD, Lead Bank hotute bakah Khawzawl district chhunga bank hrang hrang aiawhte an tel a ni.

Attachment

title: KHAWZAWLA CHILD CARE INSTITUTIONS (CCIS) (NAUPANG ENKAWLNA INTE) ENDIKNA NEIH A NI
content:
1045/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 17th June, 2026:Vawiin khan Khawzawl District Level Inspection Committee (DLIC) te chu an Chairman, Addl. Deputy Commissioner ni bawk, Pu K. Zorammuana hovin Khawzawla naupang enkawlna in, Child Care Institutions (CCIs) awmte tlawhin, an hanthawh leh hmalaknate an endik.

Khawzawla home hrang hrang awm - Khawzawl Observation Home, Exodus Boys Home leh Little Woods Girls Home te chu tlawh kual niin, CCI Staff-te chu an hnathawh chin leh an hnathawh dante, an care plan te, naupang an enkawl dante an enpui bakah hun an hman dan te an sawipui bawk a. An harsatna leh hmachhawpte, puih an ngaihna lai leh an hnathawh dana duhkhawp lohna an neih te sawipuiin inspection hlawkthlak tak neih a ni.

DLIC member-te chuan naupang harsatna hrang hrang tawkte enkawlna hmun tha a awm mai bakah a thawktuten tha taka an hna an thawk thei chu lawmawm an tih thute sawiin, theihtawp chhuaha nakin zela mi chhawrtlak leh mi rinawm ni tura naupangte kawng dika hruaia, tha taka enkawl zel turin an chah a ni.

Attachment

title: State pawn atanga Mizoram rawn tlawhtuten min fak thinna langsar chu faina leh thianghlimna hi a ni a, mi tinin kan uar zel a ngai - Pu Lalduhoma, Chief Minister
content:
1044/2026-2027

Aizawl the 17th June 2026:Formosa Foundation chuan vawiin khan Joint YMA Thenzawl hnenah bawlhhlawh paihna lirthei (Garbage Tipper) an pe a. A hlanna inkhawm hi Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan khuallian niin a hmanpui a. Pi Baryl Vanneihsangi, MLA chuan Chief Minister hi a tawiawm.

Lirthei inhlanna hun hi Joint YMA Hall, Bazar Veng Thenzawlah buatsaih a ni a. Chief Minister chuan thu sawiin, "Mizorama khualzin lo kalten min fak thinna langsar tak mai chu khawi hmun pawh a lo thianghlim viau zel hi a ni a.Faina leh thianghlimna hian kan ram leh hnam a chawimawi mek a ni. Chuvang chuan Mizoram mipuiten faina kan uar deuh deuh a ngai.

Thenzawl khua hi India ram huapa Mizoram khaw lar ber zinga mi a ni mek a, a hmunhma a remin a, khualzin pawhin an hre tawh hle a, tlawhtu pawh in ngah tial tial a. Khaw dang zawng zawng aia in lo fai bikte pawh hi a tul ve a. Chu mi hre chung chuan Formosa Foundation pawhin he lirthei hi an rawn pe che u a ni, a ti a. Faina leh thianghlimna lo uar deuh deuh tura chahin Thenzawlah home stay tam zawk siam a nih tur thu a tarlang.

Mizoram tana thil tha tam tak pe thintu Formosa Foundation te chungah lawmthu a sawi bawk.

Baryl Vanneihsangi MLA pawhin thu sawiin, faina hi hriselna bakah min tizahawmtu a ni a. Thenzawl hian paltlangtu leh tlawhtu a ngah avangin fai taka enkawl hi mi dangte entawn atan pawh a tul a ni, a ti. Fai leh thianghlim taka kan awm theih nan bawlhhlawh tha taka sawngbawl a pawimawh thu a tarlang bawk.

Lirthei inhlanna hi Pu Lalmuanpuia, President Joint YMA Thenzawlin a kaihruai a, Pu Samuel Lalrinpuia, Vantawng Group YMA Secretary-in inlawmna thu a sawi a, Pu Jonathan Lalremruata, Chairman, Formosa Foundation-in thu sawiin, Clinton Sherwood, Legal Advisor Formosa Foundation kaihruainain lirthei inhlanna buatsaih a ni.

Attachment

title: AIZAWL DISTRICT AH SINGLE USE PLASTIC HMAN KHAPNA TOURIST SITE 7 PUAN A NI
content:
1043/2026-2027

Dt. 17.07.2026:Urban Development & Poverty Alleviation Department (UD&PA) chuan Aizawl District chhung Tourist Site atan a hnuai ami te hi a thlang a:

1. Lalsavunga Park2. Falkawn Zokhua3. Sakawrhmuituai Tlang4. Hmuifang Tlang5. Khawhpawp, Lawipu6. Sairang Railway Station7. Sialsuk Tlang

Aizawl DC chuan heng Tourist Sites ah hian hman khat daih plastic, Single-Use Plastic (SUP) hman, hralh, sem leh khawl a khap a ni. Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026 hi hlawhtling taka kalpui a nih theih nan, bawlhlawh a paihna tur nilo a paih tih rem nan leh tullo a plastic hmanna tihtlem a nih theih nan te he thupek hi chhuah a ni.

Heng hmun tlawhtute, a huamchhunga vendor-te, dawr neitute leh he thupek in a nghawng tel tur dangte chuan SUP aiah environment tichhe theilo leh hman nawn theih hmang zawk tawh tur an ni a. Nodal Officer leh Sanitation Officer ten SUP Enforcement Team kaltlangin he Order hi zawm a nih ngei theih nan an bawhzui ang.

Heihi Supreme Court of India in dt. 05.05.2026 a Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026 kalpui naah tourist site-te thlang a, bawlhhlawh sawngbawl dante tihhmasawn a nih theih na tura thuchhuah a siam bawhzuina a ni a.

He Order bawhchhiatna a awm chuan Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026; Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016 leh a kaihhnawih dang Dan hnuaia ngaihtuah theih a ni ang.

title: Lungleiah Govt J. Buana College te'n zirlai chhuak turte thlahna hun an hmang
content:
1042/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Nikir thla ni 17:Vawiin khan Government J. Buana College, Lunglei te chuan an college Multipurpose Hall-ah an zirlai chhuak tur VI Semester te thlahna hun, 'Valedictory Day' an hmang a. Zirlai 148 thlahin, zirlai an thlahte hi Bachelor of Arts (BA) leh Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com) zirlaite an ni.

He hunah hian Vice Principal Pu Lalrotluanga chuan Principal Address sawiin, zirlaite thlahna thu a sawi a. Pi Mary Lalromawii, Head, Department of Economics chuan zirlai chhuak turte fuihna thu a sawi bawk.

Valedictory Day-ah hian zirlai chhuak tur zinga mark hmu sang ber K. Lalnunhlimi, Department of Geography chu 'Valedictorian' atana thlan niin, ani hian inthlahna thu 'Valedictory Speech' a sawi a. Zirlai chhuak tur zinga Library hmang ṭangkai ber thlan ni bawkin, Lalhmangaihzuala, Department of Public Administration chu 'Best Library User Award' hi hlan a ni.

Programme kar lakah a inchhawkin zaithiamte zaiin, College zirlai zaithiam rualte'n 'Government J. Buana College Theme Song' an sa bawk a. Zirlai kal chhuak turte zingah award hlimawm tak, 'Hall of Fame Awards' chi hrang hrang sem niin, Students' Union hruaitu kal chhuak turte ngaihhruina thilpek hlan an nih bakah, zirlai chhuak tur zawng zawngte ngaihhruina thilpek pek vek an ni bawk a. Tlai lamah 'Valedictory Day Dinner' chawhlui kil ho niin, photo session leh jam session hun hman a ni bawk.

Government J. Buana College hi kum 1983-a din niin, tunah hian zirna ṭhuang hnih – Bachelor of Arts leh Bachelor of Commerce kalpui mek a  ni a. Arts stream-ah hian department 8 awmin, commerce stream phei hi chu Lungleia sawrkar college-a commerce zirna awm chhun a ni bawk.

Government J. Buana College hi National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC)-in Grade a pek hnuhnun berah B Grade a pe a ni.

Attachment

title: LAWNGTLAI DISTRICT DISASTER MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY (DDMA) HOLDS MEETING TO PREPARE FOR UPCOMING MONSOON
content:
178/2026-2027

Lawngtlai, 17th June 2026:In preparation for the upcoming monsoon season, the Lawngtlai District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA) held a meeting today at the Office Chamber of the Chairperson and Deputy Commissioner, Shri  Donny Lalruatsanga, who chaired the meeting.

Shri Donny Lalruatsanga first expressed his appreciation to the members present. He highlighted that various disasters may occur during the monsoon and urged DDMA members to remain alert and be ready to respond whenever necessary. As landslides causing road blockages may occur, he directed road maintenance agencies including MPWD, NHIDCL, ABCI, RDS and PMPL to remain vigilant. He also stated that Quick Response Teams (QRTs) and SDRF personnel must stay prepared for rescue operations during emergencies.

The Chairperson stressed the importance of close coordination with voluntary organisations, especially during disasters, when cooperation with the Government becomes crucial. He called upon all concerned to work together effectively. He also assured the public that DDMA would continue to make every effort for the safety and welfare of the people of Lawngtlai District during the monsoon.

During the meeting, several important matters were discussed. Recognising that disasters may occur during the monsoon, the members agreed on the need for dedicated and coordinated action. They decided that immediate steps should be taken to clear roads blocked by landslides and that MPWD, NHIDCL, ABCI, RDS and PMPL should prepare proper action plans for the roads under their respective jurisdictions and remain prepared.

The meeting also emphasised the need for proper display of contact numbers and clear signage along roads maintained by the respective agencies so that the public can easily reach them during emergencies. It was further stressed that quick communication through reliable means is essential during disasters, and Telecom Service Providers should take prompt action to strengthen communication systems.

The members also highlighted the importance of strong coordination among Government departments, companies and NGOs during disaster situations and called for continued cooperation.

The meeting was attended by DDMA members in full strength. The Chairperson, Shri  Donny Lalruatsanga, concluded the meeting by expressing his gratitude.

Attachment

title: FURPUI LO THLENG TUR ATANA  INBUATSAIHNA NEIIN LAWNGTLAI DISTRICT DISASTER MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY (DDMA) THUKHAWM
content:
1041/2026-2027

Lawngtlai, 17th June 2026:Furpui lo thleng tura atana inbuatsaihna neiin Lawngtlai District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA) chuan an Chairperson leh Bawrhsap nibawk Pu Donny Lalruatsanga kaihhruaiin a Office Chamber-ah thutkhawm an nei.

Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan Member-kalkhawmte hnena lawmthu sawina a nei hmasa a. Fur lo thleng mai turah kawng hrang hranga chhiatna lo thleng thei reng a nih thu tarlangin, DDMA Member-te bengvar taka awm tur leh a tul dan a zira chechhuak thei turin a chah a. Leiminin kawng a hnawh pin chu thleng thei reng a nih angin, kawngpui enkawltu MPWD, NHIDCL, ABCI, RDS  leh PMPL- te chu inralring reng turin a chah a ni. Chhiatna thlengah chhanchhuahna hna thawk turin QRT leh SDRF - te inrâlring renga awm a pawimawh dawn hle a ni, a ti. Chairperson chuan Tlawmngai Pawl te nen thawhhona tha a awm a pawimawh tak zet tih tarlangin, a bik takin chhiatna lo thlen thulha sawrkar nen thawhho a pawimawh dawn tih sawiin, tha taka thawkho turin a sawm a. Fur lo thleng turah mipuite tan DDMA an awm reng tih sawiin Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan Lawngtlai District- mipuite tan theihtawp chhuah a nih zel tur thu a sawi a ni.

Thutkhawmah hian thil pawimawh tak tak sawiho a ni a. Furpui lo thleng turah chhiatna thleng thei reng a ni tih pawm tlangin thahnemngai taka thawhho ni se an ti a. Leiminin kawng a hnawhpin thulhah chak zawka hmalak ni se an ti a,  MPWD, NHIDCL, ABCI, RDS  leh PMPL- te chu anmahni enkawl theuh kawngah  ruahmanna fel fai taka siamin, inrâlring rengin lo awm se an ti a. A bik takin mipuiten awlsam zawka an biakpawh theih nan anmahni enkawl theuh kawnga biakna tur 'contact number' dahin  'Signage' mumal takin lo tar se an ti a ni.  Chhiatna lo thlenin biakhlatna hmanga inbiak pawh zung zung a pawimawh hle tiin, Telecom Service Provider- ten chak zawkin hma lo la se an ti a ni. Chhiatna thlen thulhah sawrkar, Company leh NGO - te inkarah thawhhona tha a pawimawh hle tiin, tha taka thawhho zel ni se an ti bawk a ni.

Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Lawngtlai District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA) Member- te an telkim tha hle a, Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan lawmthu sawiin hun a khar.

Attachment

title: Thirteen Students Become Deputy Commissioner for a Day in Lunglei Under NMBA and SIR 2026
content:
177/2026-2027

Lunglei, 17 June 2026:The Lunglei District Administration on Wednesday successfully organised "Be Deputy Commissioner for a Day 2.0", an innovative initiative of Deputy Commissioner Pi Navneet Mann, IAS, under the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA) and in convergence with the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) 2026. Held under the theme "Say No to Drugs, Yes to Democracy (Special Intensive Revision)", the programme provided thirteen students with firsthand exposure to governance and public administration while fostering awareness about substance abuse prevention and democratic participation.

The participants comprised thirteen students drawn from schools across Lunglei District. Most were academically meritorious students nominated and selected by their respective schools based on their performance and potential. In addition, one participant was a Child with Special Needs (CwSN), specially identified and nominated by education officials to ensure inclusivity in the programme. Two students were selected through a Wildcard Entry round, in which interested students submitted short write-ups explaining why they wished to participate in the initiative. The participants included Janet R. Lalremsangi (Govt Electric Veng High School), F. Lalhruaizeli (Govt Leitlangpui High School), ZS Malsawmsanga Sailo (Govt High School, Lunglei), C. Lalbiakdika (Baptist Higher Secondary School, Serkawn), Ramdinmawia (Govt Rahsiveng High School), Jenny Malsawmdawngi (Govt Bazar High School), Ruthi Lalzarmawii (Holy Faith School), Cindy B. Lalvensangi (Sacred Heart School), Lalrinpuia Fanai (Govt Venglai High School), Zomuansangi (FLKM School), Jonathan Malsawmtluanga (Bethesda Higher Secondary School), Freddy Lalawmpuia and Lalzawmliani (2nd Battalion MAP English Medium School).

The programme commenced with a Welcome and Orientation Session at the Deputy Commissioner's Office, where the students were introduced to the role and responsibilities of the Deputy Commissioner and the functioning of the district administration. This was followed by an office tour during which they visited various sections, including Planning, Establishment, Aadhaar Cell, Disaster Management and Election, and interacted with officials to gain a better understanding of their responsibilities.

A major highlight of the programme was the Mock Morning Briefing held at the DC Conference Hall. Students were divided into groups and took turns assuming the role of Deputy Commissioner while listening to presentations by various departments, including DRDO, Election, Revenue, Police, DDMA and Health. The exercise enabled the participants to understand administrative coordination, decision-making and governance processes.

The students later participated in a Press Conference at the Deputy Commissioner's Chamber, where they took turns acting as Deputy Commissioner and responded to questions from media personnel on various issues concerning governance, development and public welfare. The exercise provided valuable exposure to leadership, public communication and decision-making.

Following lunch, the participants undertook field visits to various locations, including the Health ATM at Civil Hospital, Lunglei, and the Cosmic Class initiative at Govt Leitlangpui Higher Secondary School, Lunglei. As part of the programme's special focus on democratic participation, the students were also attached to Booth Level Officers (BLOs) and observed Special Intensive Revision (SIR) activities in the field, including household visits and voter outreach efforts.

The programme concluded with a Reflection and Sharing Session, during which the students shared their experiences, discussed the challenges they encountered and reflected on the lessons they learned about leadership, governance and public service. Certificates of participation were distributed, followed by closing remarks and a group photograph.

Speaking on the occasion, Deputy Commissioner Pi Navneet Mann expressed appreciation for the enthusiasm and active participation shown by the students. She encouraged them to become ambassadors of positive change in their schools and communities by promoting both a drug-free lifestyle and active democratic participation.

The programme was widely appreciated by participants and officials alike and successfully provided young students with firsthand exposure to the responsibilities of public administration. Through its unique convergence of the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan and the Special Intensive Revision of Electoral Rolls, the initiative reinforced the message that the youth should choose leadership over addiction and democracy over drugs.

Attachment

title: NMBA leh SIR 2026 hnuaiah Lunglei Bawrhsap ruahmannain Lungleia zirlai sawmpathum te'n "Ni Khat Atan Bawrhsap" ṭhutthleng an luah
content:
1040/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Nikir thla ni 17:Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA) hnuaia ruihhlo do beihpui peng khat atan leh Special Intensive Revision (SIR) 2026 hnuaia electoral roll ennawn runpui kalpui mek hlawhtlinna atan Lunglei Deputy Commissioner (Bawrhsap) Pi Navneet Mann ruahmannain vawiin khan Lungleiah hlawhtling takin "Ni Khat Atan Bawrhsap" thawh hnihna (Be Deputy Commissioner for a Day 2.0) hun buatsaih niin, Bawrhsap hna thawh dan a taka an hriatna tur leh mawhphurhna pawimawh la ngam tura fuih phurtu atan Lunglei khawpui chhunga zirlai naupang sawmpathum chu Bawrhsap ṭhutthleng luahtir an ni.

"Say No to Drugs, Yes to Democracy (Special Intensive Revision)" [Ruihhlo hnawl la, mipui rorelna thlang rawh (SIR)] tih thupui hmanga District Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann kaihhruaia hma lakna hi an hma huna hruaitu dinhmun luah tura zirlaite cho phurtu atan leh ruihhlo tel lova an tum hlen chhuak tura tum ruhna petu atan te, electoral roll dik leh felfai a awm theihna tur leh mipui rorelna chawi nun a nih zel theih nana inzirtirna atan te ruahman a ni a. Lunglei khawpui chhunga zirna kawnga ti ṭha leh entawn tlak zirlai sawmpathum – Janet R. Lalremsangi (Govt Electric Veng High School), F. Lalhruaizeli (Govt Leitlangpui High School), ZS Malsawmsanga Sailo (Govt High School, Lunglei), C. Lalbiakdika (Baptist Higher Secondary School, Serkawn), Ramdinmawia (Govt Rahsiveng High School), Jenny Malsawmdawngi (Govt Bazar High School), Ruthi Lalzarmawii (Holy Faith School), Cindy B. Lalvensangi (Sacred Heart School), Lalrinpuia Fanai (Govt Venglai High School), Zomuansangi (FLKM School), Jonathan Malsawmtluanga (Bethesda Higher Secondary School), Freddy Lalawmpuia leh Lalzawmliani (2nd Battalion MAP English Medium School) te an tel a. A tam zawk chu sikul lamin an zirlaite zinga ti ṭha leh theihna nei an thlan chhuahte an ni a. Mi tin huap zo hma lakna a nih theih nan Education lam hotute'n vohbik zirlai, Child with Special Needs (CwSN) pakhat thlang telin, Wildcard Entry hmangin anmahni ngeiin programme-a tel an duh chhan ziaka an theh luh aṭangin zirlai pahnih thlan an ni bawk.

Zing dar 9:30-ah Bawrhsap lirthei hmangin zirlai thlan chhuah 13 te hian Bawrhsap pisa thlengin, Bawrhsap hna thawh dan hrang hrang hrilhfiah an ni a. Chumi hnuah Bawrhsap Pisaa pindan hrang hrang – Planning, Establishment, Aadhaar Cell, Disaster Management leh Election Section te fang kualin, thawktute nen an inkawm a. DC Conference Hall-ah department hrang hrang 6 – District Rural Development Office (DRDO), Election, Revenue, Police, District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA) leh Health Department te'n PowerPoint Presentation hmanga an department hna thawh leh hma lakna an tar lante ngaithlain, inkawm hona hun an hmang bawk.

Hemi hnu hian Bawrhsap hna thawh pindanah thu thar theh darhtute nen inkawmin, hemi hunah hian Bawrhsap ṭhutthlenga ṭhuin, Lunglei District hmasawnna tur hrang hrang – hmasawnna ruhrel siam, ruihhlo do, electoral roll dik leh felfai nei tura hma lak, chhiat rupna laka inven, zirna leh infiamna tihhmasawn kawnga an ngaih dan thu thar theh darhtute'n an zawh chu an chhang a. DC hma laknaa Civil Hospital Lungleia Health ATM leh Government Leitlangpui High School-a Cosmic Class te chu a hmunah tlawh a, an enfiah bakah, SIR kalpui meka electoral roll dik, felfai leh kimchang a awm theih nana Booth Level Officer (BLO)-te hna thawh leh hma lakna a hmunah hmuhpuiin, mipui rorelna (democracy) chungchanga thil pawimawh hrang hrang a takin an zir bawk.

Tlai lamah "Ni Khat Atan Bawrhsap" hun hman aṭanga Zirlai-Bawrhsapte'n an thil zir chhuah leh tawn hriatte inhrilh tawnna hun buatsaih niin, Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann chuan Zirlai-Bawrhsapte hnenah hian thuziak (certificate) leh thilpek (memento) a hlan a ni.

Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann chuan zirlaite phur taka an tel leh hlawhtling taka hun hman a ni chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. Zirlaite chu an sikul leh khawtlangah a ṭha zawnga danglamna thlentu ni tur te, an tawn hriatte ṭangkai taka hmang tur leh, ruihhlo do leh democracy pawimawhna puang darhtu palai nih tum ṭheuh turin a chah.

"Ni Khat Atan Bawrhsap" tih hi zirlaite chuan an hlawkpui thu leh thil tam tak an hriat belh thu sawiin, chhunzawm zel atan ṭha an tih thu an sawi a. Lunglei Bawrhsap pawhin Lunglei District-a zirna leh zirlaite tihhmasawnna lam hawi hma lakna kalpui chhunzawm zel turin ruahmanna an siam zel tur thu a sawi.

Attachment

title: Horticulture Minister Pu C. Lalsawivunga'n Horticulture Fair vawi 13-na a Hawng
content:
1039/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl the 17th June, 2026 :Vawiin khan Horticulture Minister Pu C. Lalsawivunga'n Horticulture Fair vawi 13-na Lammualah a hawng a, a hawnna inkhawmah hian Minister of State Prof. Lalnilawma leh MIFCO Chairman Pu Lalthazuala te pawh an tel.

Pu C. Lalsawivunga chuan Horticulture Fair in a tum ber chu kuthnathawktute’n an dehchhuah awmze neia an hralh chhuahna atan anih thu a sawi a, Aizawl khawpui leh a chheh vela chengte chu Fair a inpho te duhsak taka an thil zawrh te chu leisak turin a sawm.

Minister chuan Mizoram chu UT a nih atangin kum 50 chuang a liam tawh a, state puitling a hlankai a nih pawh kum 40 dawn a ni tawh chungin vawiinah thil siam chhuah leh deh chhuah sawi tham a la nei lo a, chu chuan Mizote chu kan la intodelh loh zia a entir, a ti a, chuvangin he Fair atang hian hmasawnna kawng kan ngaihtuah a tul a, ram leh hnam intodelh ni turin tan kan lak a ngai a ni, a ti.

Organic farming lam pawh sawiin, Pu C. Lalsawivunga chuan state dangte’n Mizoram sawhthing an duh bik chhan chu chemical tel lo, organic a nih vang a ni a, chuvangin thlai leh thei chingtute chu ‘organic’ thei ang bera ching turin a chah a, India rama lo neitu dangte’n an chin ve loh ching turin a fuih bawk. Mizoram sawrkar chuan ‘Sapthei’ hmangin pilot project kalpui chhoh a tum mek thu a sawi bawk a ni.

Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Pu C. Lalsawivunga kaihhruaina hnuaiah Horticulture Department-in nasa takin hma a sawn thu a sawi a, Mizoram chu a sik leh sain a zir avangin thei leh thlai a tha duh em em a, chuvangin Horticulture Department chu Mizoram hmasawnna atana department pawimawh berte zinga mi a nih thu a sawi.

Horticulture Fair buatsaih rual hian District tina kuthnathawktu tha ber lawmman 'Best Horticulture Farmer Award’ hlan a ni a, he award hi Fair thlengtu in a hlan a ni. Award dawngtute chu :

Horticulture Fair hi kum 2012 atang khan neih tan niin, kumin hi a vawi 13 na a ni a, Horticulture Department chuan thei leh thlai chingtute, pangpar chingtute leh a zuar thin te, thei leh thlai vawn that theih siam lama tui mite pualin a buatsaih thin a ni. He hun hi fur ruahtui Mizoramin a dawn that hun, thei leh thlai chi hrang hrang kan phun hun mil ber turin June thla ah neih ber thin a ni. Kum 2012 atanga 2025 thlenga stall neituten an hralh chhuah tawh zat belh khawm chu ₹3,76,90,607/- a ni.

Horticulture Fair 2026 hi ni li awh (17-20 June 2026) tura ruahman a ni a, bonsai siam leh enkawl dan te, greenhouse enkawl dan te, thlai venhimna damdawi hman dan te, pangpar khawi dan te leh bamboo vinegar hman dan te inzirtirna hun hman tura ruahman a ni. Stall inpho hi a vaiin 273 a awm a ni.

Horticulture Fair hawnna inkhawm hi Pu CH Lalmuanpuia, Horticulture Department Director in a kaihruai a, Pu C. Zosangliana, Additional Director chuan technical report pein, Pi Lucy Zaithangpuii, Additional Director chuan lawmthu sawinain a hawnna inkhawm hi khar a ni.

Attachment

title: Sports Minister Attends NCC U-17 Pre-Subroto Cup Final and Closing Ceremony
content:
176/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 17th June 2026:Sports & Youth Services Minister Shri Lalnghinglova Hmar attended the final match and closing ceremony of the Inter-Group Under-17 Girls Pre-Subroto Cup as the Chief Guest this morning at the Rajiv Gandhi Stadium in Mualpui. Organized by the National Cadet Corps (NCC) North Eastern Region Directorate, the tournament concluded with the Aizawl Group emerging as champions after defeating the Guwahati Group with a 2-0 victory.

Speaking at the event, the Minister highlighted that athletes from the North East continue to be India's hope and pride in the field of football. He noted that women's football is steadily progressing across the country and extended his best wishes to the participating athletes, encouraging them to reach the highest levels of the sport.

The Pre-Subroto Cup featured a competitive lineup of eight groups representing different zones across the North East region: Silchar Group, Aizawl Group, Kohima Group, Jorhat Group, Guwahati Group, Shillong Group, Tezpur Group, and Dibrugarh Group. The overall results are as follows:

1. Aizawl Group2. Guwahati Group3. Shillong Group4. Jorhat Group5. Tezpur Group6. Dibrugarh Group7. Silchar Group8. Kohima Group

Attachment

title: Sports Minister in NCC U-17 Pre-Subroto Cup Final leh Closing Ceremony a hmanpui
content:
1038/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 17th June 2026:Vawiin chawhma khan Sports & Youth Services Minister Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar chuan Rajiv Gandhi Stadium, Mualpuiah National Cadet Corps (NCC) North Eastern Region Directorate buatsaih, Inter-Group Under-17 Hmeichhe Pre-Subroto Cup Final match leh kharna inkhawm chu khual lian niin a hmanpui a. Aizawl Group-in 2-0 a Guwahati Group hnehin an champion a ni.

Minister chuan thu sawiin Hmarchhak infiammite chu football-a India ram hmachhuan ber an ni mek zel a ni a ti a. India-ah pawh Hmeichhe football chuan hma a sawn zel a, he Cup-a khel infiammite pawh level sang tak thleng ngei turin duhsakna a hlan bawk a ni.

Pre-Subroto Cup-ah hian Hmarchhak bial atangin group pariat - Silchar Group, Aizawl Group, Kohima Group, Jorhat Group, Guwahati Group, Shillong Group, Tezpur Group leh Dibrugarh Group te an inel a. Result kimchang hetiang hi a ni:

1. Aizawl Group2. Guwahati Group3. Shillong Group4. Jorhat Group5. Tezpur Group6. Dibrugarh Group7. Silchar Group8. Kohima Group

title: Minister Pi Lalrinpuii’n World Blood Donors Day hmanpui
content:
1037/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 17th June, 2026:World Blood Donors Day, kum tina June ni 14-a hman thin chu remchan zawkna avangin vawiin June ni-17 hian BSI Hall, Khatla-ah hman niin, “One drop of humanity, give blood, save lives” chu thupuia hman a ni. Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan khuallian niin a hmanpui.

Khuallian Pi Lalrinpuii chuan Mizo nun ze mawi ‘tlawmngaihna’ chu vawn nun zel a pawimawh thu sawiin, thisen pek hi tlawmngaihna lanchhuahna a nih thu a sawi. Mizoram pawna awm Mizote’n midangte nun chhan nana thisen an pe thin chu a chhuanawm a ti a, hmun danga awmte’n Mizo nun mawi an hriat theihna pawimawh a nih thu a sawi bawk. Mizoram chhungah mimal tlawmngai leh pawl thahnemngaite’n thisen an pe thin hi fakawm tiin, chhunzawm zel turin a duh thu a sawi bawk.

Minister chuan khawvel chu damdawi leh zirna lamah hmasawn mah se, thisen siam dan an la hmuhchhuah loh avangin thisen pek leh a petu te chu an hlu em em a ni a ti a, thisen pek chu uar zual turin kalkhawmte chu a chah bawk a ni.Thisen pek tluk zetin thisen thianghlim a pawimawh thu sawiin, mitinte chu nun uluk tur leh hriselna atana pawi thei ruihhlo te, mipat hmeichhiatna thianghlim lo te lakah invawng tur leh fihlim turin mipuite chu a chah a ni.

Minister chuan mipui an pun zel avangin damlo leh thisen mamawhte pawh an pung ve zel a, thisen pe thin mi tlawmngai mimal leh pawlte chu an hlut zual thu a sawi a, mi hrisel tan thisen hi thla thum danah a pek zel theih avangin vawikhat thisen pekah duh tawk mai lovin ‘regular blood donor’ nih tum turin kalkhawmte chu a sawm bawk.

Inkhawmah hian Dr. Jane Rinsangi Ralte, Project Director, MSACS atangin presentation hmangin report ngaihthlak niin, Aizawl District chhunga Blood Center pathum- Civil Hospital Aizawl, Synod Hospital Durtlang leh ZMC & H Falkawn a thisen pe tha te hnenah chawimawina pek an ni bawk.

Chawimawina dawngtute chu-

title: Mamit District Level Committee on PMDDKY thukhawm
content:
1036/2026-2027

Dated : Mamit the 17th of June, 2026 :Nimin tlai khan Mamit District Level Committee on Prime Minister Dhan Dhaanya Krishi Yojona (PMDDKY) chu DC Conference Hall, Mamit-ah Pu Suvigya S Chandra, District Bawrhsap hovin thutkhawm a ni.

Vawiin thutkhawmah hian PMDDKY nihna leh a tum te sawi lan a ni a,  PMDDKY Portal Data submit tawhte review neih niin, Line Department hrang hrang te'n a data thehluh leh awmzia te an sawiho a ni.

District Bawrhsap chuan Line Department te hmasawn zel dan tur leh PMDDKY chu Aspirational District Mamit tana tangkai leh hmasawn pui tlaka department scheme hrang hrangte thawh tlan a duh thu a sawi.

Pi C. Vanthangzuali, ADAO, Mamit chuan Mamit District chhunga department hrang hrang hnathawh leh data an neih te a tarlang bawk.

Attachment

title: Hnahthial District Magistrate in duh duh na hmun a leivung paih khapna thupek a ti chhuak
content:
1035/2026-2027

Dated Hnahthial the 17thJune, 2026:Nimin 16thJune, 2026 khan Pi K. Vanlalruati, MCS, District Magistrate, Hnahthial District chuan District chhunga leivung duh duh na hmuna paih khapna thupek a ti chhuak.

ORDER

(Under Section 163 of Bhartiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023)

Hnahthial khawpuiah tun hnaiah kawngthlang/kawngsirah leivung paih hmuh tur a tam hle mai a, hei hi nakina thil pawi thlen thei niin, lui tui te ti nuin, tui pump kawngahte thleng harsatna a thlen thei a ni.

Chuvangin, kei, K. VANLALRUATI, MCS, District Magistrate, Hnahthial District hian Section 163 of Bhartiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 - in thuneihna min pek angin thupek ka chhuah e:

"Hnahthial District chhunga Inhmun lai leh leivung paih tur reng rengin duh duhna hmunah paih phal a ni lo, a paihna tura ruat bikah te chauh paih tur a ni."

He thupek zawm lo chuSection 223 of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023dan hmangin hrem theih a ni ang.

He thupek hian tul bik thila tihdanglam a nih loh chuan tihchhuah a nih atangathla 2 (hnih)chhung a huam ang.

He thupek hi Court Seal nemin vawiin, 16thJune, 2026 hian hming ka ziah hnan e.

Sd/-

(K.VANLALRUATI)

District Magistrate

Hnahthial District, Hnahthial

Attachment
